interpretation_summary: The replication faithfully followed the preregistered plan after converting an interactive RMarkdown into a batch script. Using a much larger Prolific sample (N≈474 vs. original N=101), the independent-samples t-test showed that contingent-feedback participants achieved higher 1D accuracy than full-information participants (M=0.771 vs 0.697). The estimated mean difference of 0.073 (95 % CI [0.044, 0.103]) closely matches the original study’s 0.08 difference (CI [0.03, 0.14]). Both intervals exclude zero and the direction is identical, indicating strong replication success.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Full alignment: the executed code computed participant-level 1D accuracy scores during the test phase and applied an independent-samples t-test exactly as preregistered. Variable names, filtering (phase == "Test"), and statistical thresholds adhered to the plan.

deviations.issue_description: Original interactive RMarkdown could not be run non-interactively; authors refactored analysis into participant_test_summary.R and a bash wrapper.
deviations.impact: Low: purely technical; statistical procedure and dataset unchanged.

deviations.issue_description: Replication used a Prolific sample rather than the original MTurk pool.
deviations.impact: Low–moderate: could influence generalisability, but does not affect internal test of hypothesis.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Participants in the contingent-feedback condition will have higher mean 1D strategy accuracy at test than participants in the full-information condition.
results_comparison.original_results: Mean 1D accuracy: contingent 0.83, full-information 0.75; mean difference ≈ 0.08 with 95 % Bayesian credible interval [0.03, 0.14] (excludes 0).
results_comparison.replication_results: Mean 1D accuracy: contingent 0.771, full-information 0.697; mean difference 0.073 (SE 0.015), 95 % CI [0.044, 0.103], t(472)=4.92, p=1.2×10⁻⁶, Cohen’s d ≈ 0.45.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Yes – replication meets preregistered criteria (p<.05, correct direction) and effect size overlaps the original estimate.

replication_report: Replication successful: contingent feedback increased reliance on the inferior 1D rule by ~0.07 (p<0.001), closely mirroring the original 0.08 effect and reinforcing the attentional-learning-trap claim.

failure_handling.suggestions: Provide a non-interactive script alongside any RMarkdown notebooks to facilitate automated replication pipelines.
Include generated output files (e.g., t-test CSV, figures) in version control to aid quick verification.

notes: Larger sample narrowed the CI, increasing precision. Future work could assess robustness across platforms (MTurk vs Prolific) and explore potential moderating demographics.
